commands: set -e && GHIDRA_URL=$(curl -fsSL https://api.github.com/repos/NationalSecurityAgency/ghidra/releases/latest | grep -oP '"browser_download_url":\s*"\K[^"]*\.zip' | head -1) && rm -rf /tmp/ghidra /opt/ghidra-* && mkdir -p /tmp/ghidra && curl -fsSLo /tmp/ghidra/ghidra.zip "$GHIDRA_URL" && unzip -qo /tmp/ghidra/ghidra.zip -d /opt && GHIDRA_DIR=$(find /opt -maxdepth 1 -type d -name 'ghidra_*' | sort | tail -1) && ln -sf "$GHIDRA_DIR/ghidraRun" /usr/local/bin/ghidra && rm -rf /tmp/ghidra

set -e && apt-get install -y git build-essential cmake meson ninja-build pkg-config libssl-dev libzip-dev liblz4-dev && rm -rf /tmp/rizin && git clone --depth=1 https://github.com/rizinorg/rizin.git /tmp/rizin && meson setup /tmp/rizin/build /tmp/rizin && meson compile -C /tmp/rizin/build && meson install -C /tmp/rizin/build && ldconfig && rm -rf /tmp/rizin

set -e && if [ "$(uname -m)" != "x86_64" ]; then echo "Cutter AppImage upstream is only provided for x86_64 in this installer" >&2; exit 1; fi && TAG=$(curl -Ls -o /dev/null -w %{url_effective} https://github.com/rizinorg/cutter/releases/latest | awk -F/ '{print $NF}') && curl -fsSLo /usr/local/bin/cutter "https://github.com/rizinorg/cutter/releases/download/${TAG}/Cutter-${TAG}-Linux-x86_64.AppImage" && chmod +x /usr/local/bin/cutter

packages.name: IDA Pro 9.3 SP1
packages.description: Commercial disassembler (manual install after dependency prep)

commands: set -e && apt-get update && apt-get install -y libxcb-cursor0 libxcb-icccm4 libxcb-image0 libxcb-keysyms1 libxcb-randr0 libxcb-render-util0 libxcb-shape0 libxcb-xfixes0 libxcb-xkb1 libxkbcommon-x11-0 libx11-6 libx11-xcb1 libxext6 libxrender1 libxi6 libxrandr2 libxcb1 libxcb-glx0 libxcb-shm0 libxkbcommon0 libfontconfig1 libfreetype6 libglib2.0-0 libdbus-1-3 libnss3 libegl1 libgl1 && (apt-get install -y libasound2t64 || apt-get install -y libasound2)
linux.note: Dependencies for Linux IDA were installed. Now install IDA manually from your archive: 1) rename the installer to a sane filename if needed, for example ida-pro_93_x64linux.run, 2) chmod +x ida-pro_93_x64linux.run, 3) run ./ida-pro_93_x64linux.run from your GUI session, 4) install into the default path ~/ida-pro-9.3, 5) launch ~/ida-pro-9.3/ida. If you want the full step-by-step instructions, open docs/ida_linux_manual.md.

windows.note: Install IDA manually from your user-supplied archive. See docs/ida_linux_manual.md for the general workflow and adapt it for Windows.

commands: if [ -d /opt/pwndbg ]; then cd /opt/pwndbg && git pull 2>/dev/null; else git clone --depth=1 https://github.com/pwndbg/pwndbg /opt/pwndbg; fi && cd /opt/pwndbg && ./setup.sh

set -e && JADX_URL=$(curl -fsSL https://api.github.com/repos/skylot/jadx/releases/latest | grep -oP '"browser_download_url":\s*"\K[^"]*jadx[^"]*\.zip' | grep -iv windows | grep -iv gui | head -1) && rm -rf /opt/jadx /tmp/jadx-extract && mkdir -p /tmp/jadx-extract && curl -fsSLo /tmp/jadx.zip "$JADX_URL" && unzip -qo /tmp/jadx.zip -d /tmp/jadx-extract && JADXD=$(find /tmp/jadx-extract -maxdepth 1 -type d -name 'jadx*' | head -1) && mkdir -p /opt/jadx && cp -a "$JADXD"/. /opt/jadx/ && chmod +x /opt/jadx/bin/* 2>/dev/null || true && ln -sf /opt/jadx/bin/jadx /usr/local/bin/jadx && ln -sf /opt/jadx/bin/jadx-gui /usr/local/bin/jadx-gui && rm -rf /tmp/jadx-extract /tmp/jadx.zip
set -e && rm -rf /tmp/pycdc && git clone --depth=1 https://github.com/zrax/pycdc /tmp/pycdc && cd /tmp/pycdc && cmake . && make -j$(nproc) && install pycdc pycdas /usr/local/bin/ && rm -rf /tmp/pycdc
